meta.title: Warehouse Racking & MHE Safety Assessment — <named site / installation>
meta.subtitle: EN 15635 inspection regime · SEMA RAG remedial priority · engineered MHE/pedestrian segregation
meta.author: HSE Logistics & Transport
meta.deid_notice: Personnel and any prior struck-by / rack-collapse incident are referred to by role label only. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: <One-paragraph summary: the named site/installation, the EN 15635 inspection-regime status, the headline SEMA RAG findings (Red = immediate off-load), and the engineered MHE/pedestrian segregation gaps. State that controls are SWL-design / inspection / engineered-segregation led.>

sections.caption: Racking configuration vs displayed SWL — an unsupplied SWL is [GAP], never assumed

rows.title: <Red-band damage finding>
rows.description: SEMA Red — critical. Immediate off-load + isolate the affected run; repair before reuse.

sections.text: <Describe the traffic layout and whether vehicles and pedestrians are ENGINEERED apart by barriers, one-way systems, and marked/protected walkways/crossings — before any reliance on hi-vis or signage.>
Any SEMA Red-band finding triggers an IMMEDIATE off-load + isolate of the affected run — it is never down-rated to 'monitor'. Amber repairs within 4 weeks and auto-escalates to Red.

items: BS EN 15635:2008 + SEMA Code of Practice (racking inspection regime + RAG)
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.178 / PUWER 1998 / HSE ACOP L117 / HSG136 (MHE + engineered segregation)
India: Motor Transport Workers Act 1961 / Factories Act via hse-india (state detection; [GAP], no national form)
